qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Stack overflow in DOMParser via deeply nested XML triggering recursive normalize()
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets recursive Document.normalize() traversal after DOMParser.parseFromString() on deeply nested XML. The advisory targets unvalidated Comment node serialization in XMLSerializer, where createComment-controlled data containing comment-breaking sequences is emitted into XML markup.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying final finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The only final qualifying finding concerns stack overflow from unbounded recursive normalize() calls during XML parsing, while GHSA-j759-j44w-7fr8 concerns XML node injection through unsafe serialization of attacker-controlled Comment node data.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-j759-j44w-7fr8 describes createComment()/Comment data reaching XMLSerializer.serializeToString(), which concatenates comment delimiters with node.data and permits injected XML nodes when the data contains comment-breaking sequences.
The final session output lists one materialized vulnerability folder: stack_overflow_normalize.

stack_overflow_normalize/vulnerability_report.json describes DOMParser.parseFromString() automatically calling doc.normalize(), whose recursive child.normalize() traversal over deeply nested XML causes RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded.
The finding does not describe Comment nodes, XMLSerializer comment serialization, missing well-formedness validation for comment data, or injection of XML nodes through serialized comment output.
